Done
Confirmed green first: the dump shows the /account/enroll-totp 500 was already fixed, and your terminal run is clean (431 tests, 0 failed; smoke + quick tunnel healthy). So we're clear to proceed.

M3 Slice 2 — table-join tokens: generation, validation, metric, admin QR fallback
Extract at the repo root (tar xzf m3-slice2-join-tokens.tar.gz); every path drops straight into place.

Files to delete: none.

What landed
DapperTableJoinSecretReader (DataAccess) — the only path to the server-only join_secret, kept behind its own narrow interface (the deliberate counterpart to ITableDirectory, which never selects it). The read is gated on is_active = true, so §4.1's "deactivating stops validation and rendering" lives in one SQL predicate that both paths flow through.
TableJoinTokens (WebApplication) — the app-layer wrapper over the already-vector-tested domain JoinTokenService. DescribeCurrentAsync builds the current QR (token, scan URL, inline server-side SVG, next-rotation instant); ValidateAsync maps a presented token to the domain result and records table_join_tokens_validated_total{result} on every attempt (missing/inactive table → invalid).
/administration/tables/{id}/join-code — a static-SSR page realizing the §4.5 / §11.4 counter-admin fallback: renders a table's rotating QR when its display is down, with the scan URL, next-rotation time, and a "Show a fresh code" refresh. Reached from a new Join code section on ManageTable.
Wired both new services into AddRestaurantTables(); extended TablesWiringTests (+2 facts) and added TableJoinSecretReaderTests (Testcontainers, 4 facts) and TableJoinTokensTests (in-memory stub reader + fixed clock, 6 facts).
Why this is small
The metric and all three config knobs (TABLE_JOIN_TOKEN_ROTATION_SECONDS, etc., with §13 bounds) were already scaffolded in the M1 infra, and the entire M3 schema ships in 0001. So no migration, no new packages, and no spec/ADR edits — this purely implements behavior §4.3/§4.4/§4.5/§12 already describe.

Deliberately deferred (next slice)
The join grant cookie, the /table/{id} GET routing (member vs anonymous vs valid-token), sitting creation + membership, and SittingMemberJoined — plus display pairing and /display. Those pull in the whole guest-registration surface. TableJoinTokens.ValidateAsync is the exact hook that flow will call.

One file to hand-append (not in the tar, per its size)
Add this under the "M3 Slice 1" entry in docs/BUILD_PROGRESS.md:

markdown
### M3 Slice 2 — table join tokens: generation, validation, metric + admin QR fallback (landed)

The rotating join-token machinery (ADR-0009, §4.3–§4.5) is now wired end-to-end on the
server side. New server-only `ITableJoinSecretReader`/`DapperTableJoinSecretReader`
(DataAccess/Tables) reads a table's `join_secret` for token work — the deliberate narrow
counterpart to `ITableDirectory`, which never selects it — gated on `is_active = true`, so
§4.1's "deactivating stops validation and rendering" is one SQL predicate both paths share.
New `ITableJoinTokens`/`TableJoinTokens` (WebApplication/Tables) wraps the vector-tested
domain `JoinTokenService`: `DescribeCurrentAsync` builds the current QR (token, scan URL,
server-side inline SVG, next-rotation instant) for the counter/admin fallback and the future
display; `ValidateAsync` maps a presented token to the domain result and records
`table_join_tokens_validated_total{result}` (§12) on every attempt — a missing/inactive table
or malformed/old token is counted `invalid`, keeping the label set to §4.3's
{valid|expired|invalid}. One static-SSR admin page `/administration/tables/{id}/join-code`
renders the fallback QR on demand (§4.5/§11.4), reached from a new "Join code" section on
ManageTable; a deactivated/unknown table renders no code. Both services registered by
`AddRestaurantTables()` (scoped, alongside the management services). No migration
(restaurant_table ships in 0001), no packages (Dapper + Net.Codecrete.QrCodeGenerator + the
framework meter, all present), no spec edit (this realizes already-specified behaviour; the
metric and TABLE_JOIN_* config were scaffolded in M1). Tests: `TableJoinSecretReaderTests`
(Testcontainers, 4 facts — exact stored bytes, unknown→null, deactivated→null, rotation
tracked so the old token dies) and `TableJoinTokensTests` (in-memory, 6 facts — current QR
shape, no-secret→null, current/previous window valid, garbage/no-secret invalid), plus two
resolvability facts added to `TablesWiringTests`.

Deferred to the next M3 slice: the join **grant** cookie, `/table/{id}` GET routing
(member / anonymous / valid-token), sitting open + membership, and the `SittingMemberJoined`
broadcast (§4.4/§5.1) — `TableJoinTokens.ValidateAsync` is the hook they consume — followed by
display pairing + device auth + `/display` (§4.2/§11.5).
Say please continue and I'll take the join grant + /table/{id} flow + sitting/membership next.


M3 slice2 join tokens.tar
GZ 

Want to be notified when Claude responds?






Claude is AI and can make mistakes. Please double-check responses.
